scientific_name: # This file adds an interpretive layer to MetaMerge heatmaps showing
# how plausible each taxon's detection is given the study region.
# It is OPTIONAL — heatmaps render normally without it.
# 1. Copy this file and rename it for your project
# 2. Add rows for taxa you want to annotate.
#    Taxa absent from this file are shown without a plausibility symbol.
# 3. Pass the file to MetaMerge when rendering:
# very_likely          — Taxon is well-documented at / near the site;
# plausible            — Taxon is ecologically possible at the site
#                        but not documented at the site itself.
# unlikely             — Taxon is geographically or ecologically improbable
#                        at the site; detection requires extra scrutiny.
# likely_false_positive — Taxon is outside the known range entirely
#                        (wrong continent / biome) or is a common BLAST
#                        misassignment artefact. Treat as a false positive
# Matching is by exact scientific_name string (case-sensitive).
# You can annotate at any rank — species
# A genus entry does NOT automatically apply to child species;
# add explicit rows for any ranks you want annotated.
# programmatically using taxonomy-based rules that classify by:
# Start with manual entries for key interpreted taxa and expand with
# rules for background coverage. See the Crawford Lake example in
# the associated publication for one approach.
# Example entries (delete these and add your own):
